messages.content:
Chain-of-Thought (CoT) was first introduced in the text-only setting to elicit step-by-step reasoning in LLMs~\citep{wei2022chain}, and it was soon extended to multimodal reasoning with dedicated resources~\citep{shao2024visual}. In the line of \textit{thinking about images}, Vision-R1~\citep{huang2025vision} strengthens MLLMs’ multi-step visual reasoning via reasoning-centric training with RL-style optimization, while R1-OneVision~\citep{yang2025r1} scales this paradigm toward broader vision tasks and data coverage. However, these methods still perform reasoning in text space, which remains an indirect and inefficient representation of visual understanding.  
In parallel, ~\textit{thinking with images} relies on tool use during reasoning: Visual Sketchpad~\citep{hu2024visual} generates executable drawing/annotation programs to create intermediate visual aids, and DeepEyes~\citep{zheng2025deepeyes} predicts regions and invokes zoom/crop tools for iterative, fine-grained grounding. Moreover, some work using unified generation-and-understanding models interleave modalities within a single sequence: Chameleon~\citep{team2024chameleon} adopts a unified token interface that can emit both text and image outputs, MVoT~\citep{li2025imagine} learns to produce interleaved multimodal trajectories, and Bagel~\citep{deng2025emerging} further advances unified multimodal modeling for more general vision-language capabilities. 润色

Chain-of-Thought (CoT) was first introduced in the text-only setting to elicit step-by-step reasoning in LLMs~\citep{wei2022chain}, and it was soon extended to multimodal reasoning with dedicated resources~\citep{shao2024visual}. Along the line of *thinking about images*, Vision-R1~\citep{huang2025vision} enhances MLLMs’ multi-step visual reasoning through reasoning-centric training with RL-style optimization, while R1-OneVision~\citep{yang2025r1} scales this paradigm to broader vision tasks and more diverse data. However, these methods still conduct reasoning primarily in text space, which can be indirect and inefficient as a representation of visual understanding.

In parallel, *thinking with images* emphasizes tool use during reasoning. Visual Sketchpad~\citep{hu2024visual} generates executable drawing/annotation programs to create intermediate visual aids, and DeepEyes~\citep{zheng2025deepeyes} predicts regions and invokes zoom/crop tools for iterative, fine-grained grounding. Moreover, recent unified generation-and-understanding models interleave modalities within a single sequence: Chameleon~\citep{team2024chameleon} adopts a unified token interface that can emit both text and image outputs; MVoT~\citep{li2025imagine} learns to produce interleaved multimodal trajectories; and Bagel~\citep{deng2025emerging} further advances unified multimodal modeling toward more general vision-language capabilities.
